title: CIP Spark
eligibility_notes: Company Eligibility: Company age less than 5 years Accumulated revenue below RM3 million For less than 51% Malaysian ownership, majority employees must be Malaysian Must have IP rights or valid licensed IP arrangement No other company/entity with 25% or more shareholding No paid-up capital requirement Individual Eligibility: Minimum 2 individuals in a team (at least 1 Malaysian) Minimum 1 individual residing in Malaysia (Malaysian or non-Malaysian)

raw_item.name: CIP Spark
individual_eligibility: Minimum 2 individuals in a team (at least 1 Malaysian)
Minimum 1 individual residing in Malaysia (Malaysian or non-Malaysian)
company_eligibility: For less than 51% Malaysian ownership, majority employees must be Malaysian
Must have IP rights or valid licensed IP arrangement
No other company/entity with 25% or more shareholding
raw_item.fund_utilisation_rule: At least 60% for developmental expenses and up to 40% for non-developmental/commercialisation expenses.
raw_item.disbursement_method: Advance payment and reimbursement, subject to milestones.
deliverables: Functional prototype or MVP by end of funding
raw_item.application_process_note: Typical timeline after pitching is around 2 to 4 months, subject to complete documentation.

title: Malaysia Digital Catalyst Grant (MDCG)
description: Catalyse co-creation, problem-solving, development and commercialisation of disruptive and sustainable solutions with end-user partners within Malaysia Digital promoted sectors.
eligibility_notes: Eligibility: Incorporated in Malaysia under Companies Act 1965/2016 Minimum issued share capital RM50,000 At least 51% Malaysian equity Incorporated in Malaysia under Companies Act 1965/2016 Minimum issued share capital RM500,000 At least 1 year in operation Must not be under liquidation/winding-up/bankruptcy Must declare litigation/blacklist/conflict disclosures Must be MD or MSC Malaysia status company No clawback dues to MDEC from prior grants Specific Conditions: Applicant/recipient must have no ownership/control relationship with end-user partner Project must demonstrate new IP creation and/or commercial value

raw_item.name: Malaysia Digital Catalyst Grant (MDCG)
raw_item.objective: Catalyse co-creation, problem-solving, development and commercialisation of disruptive and sustainable solutions with end-user partners within Malaysia Digital promoted sectors.
tech_enablers: Artificial Intelligence and Big Data Analytics
Integrated Circuit design and embedded software
Advanced network connectivity and telecommunication technology

grant_amount.local_owned_company: Up to 50% of total project cost or RM1,000,000 (whichever is lower)
grant_amount.majority_foreign_owned_company: Up to 30% of total project cost or RM1,000,000 (whichever is lower)

local_company: Incorporated in Malaysia under Companies Act 1965/2016
majority_foreign_company: Incorporated in Malaysia under Companies Act 1965/2016
other: Must be MD or MSC Malaysia status company
No clawback dues to MDEC from prior grants

specific_conditions: Applicant/recipient must have no ownership/control relationship with end-user partner
Project must demonstrate new IP creation and/or commercial value
application_documents_required: Proof of completion/non-overlap for past/on-going government grants
application_process: Register and log in to Malaysia Digital Platform
Create New Application and select Grant
Fill form and upload required supporting documents

title: National Technology & Innovation Sandbox Fund (NTIS Fund)
description: NTIS provides a live sandbox environment for innovators to test products/services/business models with regulatory relaxations; MTDC funds eligible NTIS activities.
eligibility_notes: Basic Eligibility: Malaysian individual or Malaysian-owned company Must be successfully accepted into NTIS Application Hint: Apply via NTIS application website

raw_item.name: National Technology & Innovation Sandbox Fund (NTIS Fund)
raw_item.introduction: NTIS provides a live sandbox environment for innovators to test products/services/business models with regulatory relaxations; MTDC funds eligible NTIS activities.
basic_eligibility: Must be successfully accepted into NTIS
types_of_scheme: Single Site Sandbox: 100% grant up to RM250,000; 6 months
Multiple Site Sandbox: up to 70% grant capped at RM500,000; 6 months
Commercialisation Sandbox: up to 70% (grant + CPN) capped at RM4 million; 18 months
Corporate Sandbox: 1:1 matching funding with approved corporate partner; duration based on project
scope_of_funding: For sandbox 3/4: machinery, equipment, marketing and promotional activities
raw_item.funding_period: Sandbox 1/2 up to 6 months, Sandbox 3 up to 18 months, Sandbox 4 depends on scope

title: Halal Technology Development Fund (HTDF)
description: Hybrid matching funding (Grant + Convertible Promissory Note) for technology-enabled halal products/services for local and export markets.
eligibility_notes: Basic Eligibility: Company incorporated under Companies Act 1965 At least 60% Malaysian ownership Qualifies as SME (SME Corp Malaysia guideline) Has halal certification from JAKIM Not a subsidiary of GLC/MNC Demonstrates business sustainability and viability HTDF 2 additionally requires products/services already exported

raw_item.name: Halal Technology Development Fund (HTDF)
raw_item.introduction: Hybrid matching funding (Grant + Convertible Promissory Note) for technology-enabled halal products/services for local and export markets.
sub_schemes: HTDF 1: business expansion for local halal market
HTDF 2: business expansion for international halal market
basic_eligibility: Qualifies as SME (SME Corp Malaysia guideline)
HTDF 2 additionally requires products/services already exported
funding_quantum: HTDF 1 up to RM2.0 million (grant + CPN)
HTDF 2 up to RM4.0 million (grant + CPN)
raw_item.project_duration: 6 years total (2 years implementation + 4 years repayment)
